name: Thomson Reuters Westlaw API
what_it_does: Thomson Reuters Westlaw REST API for legal research, case law, statutes, regulations, and legal analytics. Enables AI agents to manage case law research and precedent analysis for litigation support automation, handle statutory and regulatory research for compliance automation, access KeyCite citation analysis for legal authority validation, retrieve secondary sources (law reviews, treatises, practice guides) for comprehensive legal research, manage court docket and filing tracking for litigation management, handle legal analytics and outcome prediction for litigation strategy, access international law and comparative legal research for global legal operations, retrieve regulatory compliance tracking and legislative monitoring for compliance automation, manage legal brief and document research workflow for legal practice automation, and integrate Westlaw with legal practice management, document management, and e-billing systems.
use_cases: Researching case law from litigation support agents
Validating legal citations from legal accuracy agents
Tracking regulatory changes from compliance monitoring agents
Analyzing litigation outcomes from legal analytics agents
not_for: Non-legal research without case law or regulatory focus
Small firms without Westlaw subscription budget
Consumer legal services without attorney oversight
best_when: A law firm, corporate legal department, or legal technology company with Westlaw access wants AI agents to automate legal research, citation validation, regulatory monitoring, and litigation analytics.
avoid_when: UNAUTHORIZED PRACTICE OF LAW RISK: Automated legal research output presented as legal advice or legal conclusions without attorney review creates unauthorized practice of law (UPL) risk; AI-generated legal research must be characterized as research tools, not attorney-client legal advice. Citation accuracy — KeyCite negative treatment alerts (overruled, distinguished, limited) must be monitored; automated legal briefs citing overruled precedent create malpractice risk. Westlaw AI hallucination risk — AI-assisted research features in Westlaw can generate hallucinated citations; automated citation checking must validate that cited cases actually exist and say what is claimed. LexisNexis vs Westlaw coverage — not all cases, statutes, or regulations appear in Westlaw; automated legal research must not assume comprehensive coverage for all jurisdictions and time periods.

auth.notes: Westlaw uses OAuth 2.0 via Thomson Reuters Developer Portal. REST API with JSON. Documentation at developer.thomsonreuters.com. Eagan, Minnesota HQ (Thomson Reuters Legal). Westlaw founded 1975. Part of Thomson Reuters (TRI NYSE). Public company (Toronto/NYSE). Revenue: $7B+ (Thomson Reuters). 500,000+ legal professionals. Dominant US legal research database with Westlaw Edge AI features. Competes with LexisNexis for legal research.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — subscription required; transactional pricing for some products
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual firm subscription; per-search or per-document pricing tiers
pricing.notes: Eagan, Minnesota. Thomson Reuters subsidiary (TRI NYSE). Westlaw founded 1975. 500,000+ legal professionals. Dominant legal research database. Competes with LexisNexis.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; search throughput documented per agreement

known_agent_gotchas: UPL UNAUTHORIZED PRACTICE OF LAW: AI-generated legal research output must be characterized as research assistance, not legal advice; autonomous legal conclusions distributed without attorney review create unauthorized practice of law risk and malpractice exposure

Citation hallucination validation — Westlaw AI features can generate research summaries with incorrect or hallucinated citations; automated legal brief preparation must verify every cited case exists and supports the proposition cited

KeyCite negative treatment monitoring — automated research must check KeyCite for negative treatment (overruled, distinguished, questioned, limited) before citing authority; citing overruled precedent creates legal malpractice risk
No webhooks — legislative and regulatory change monitoring requires polling; no native push notification for new case law, statute amendments, or regulatory changes

Coverage gaps by jurisdiction and time period — Westlaw coverage varies by jurisdiction, court level, and historical time period; automated research for older cases or specialized jurisdictions may have coverage gaps

Westlaw API access licensing — Westlaw API access requires separate licensing beyond standard research subscription; verify API access terms in firm Westlaw agreement before building production integrations
agent_readiness.security_notes: Legal research. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2 with scopes. US/EU. Case law and legal document data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Legal research. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. US/EU. Case law and legal research data.
